entities.name: RICE
entities.description: Lowland or wetland rice grown in submerged soils

entities.name: NITROGEN MINERALIZATION
entities.description: The process of converting organic nitrogen to inorganic forms such as ammonium

entities.name: NITROGEN AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen accessible for plant uptake in the soil

entities.name: WETLAND PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of biological production in wetland ecosystems

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY
entities.description: The state of the environment, impacted by nitrogen processes

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil, characterized by its quantity and quality

entities.name: MINERALIZABLE NITROGEN
entities.description: The portion of organic nitrogen that can be converted to inorganic forms

entities.name: PROLONGED SUBMERGENCE
entities.description: The state of soil being underwater for an extended period

entities.name: WETLAND SOILS
entities.description: Soils found in wetland environments, often used for rice production
